name: Princess Grace Death
description: The death of Princess Grace of Monaco (formerly Grace Kelly), an American actress who married Prince Rainier III, in a car accident in 1982, has been the subject of various conspiracy theories. These theories often question the official account that Princess Grace lost control of her car due to a stroke or other medical event, leading to the crash. Proponents suggest alternative scenarios, including that she was murdered, that her daughter Princess Stéphanie was driving (and covered up), or that the crash was engineered. Key evidence cited often revolves around inconsistencies in witness statements, the condition of the car, and alleged motives of various parties. The mainstream perspective, based on investigations by Monegasque and French authorities, attributes the accident to a stroke or transient ischemic attack suffered by Princess Grace while driving. Medical experts and accident reconstruction specialists generally support this conclusion, citing evidence from the autopsy and the crash scene. Critics of the conspiracy theories point to the lack of credible evidence supporting alternative scenarios and emphasize the thoroughness of the official investigation. The intense media scrutiny surrounding the accident and the high profile of the individuals involved have contributed to the enduring popularity of these theories.
subtitle: Conspiracy surrounding Grace Kelly's death

claims.claim: Princess Stéphanie was driving and the accident was covered up.
claims.evidence: Stéphanie's initial reluctance to discuss the accident, rumors of a fight between mother and daughter, and alleged inconsistencies in her statements.
claims.criticism: Stéphanie has consistently denied driving. Accident reconstruction and witness testimonies largely support Grace as the driver. This theory lacks concrete evidence.

claims.evidence: Alleged connections between the Monaco royal family and organized crime, potential motives related to financial or political disputes, and claims of suspicious activity before the accident.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence links the royal family to criminal activity related to Grace's death. The motives suggested are speculative and unsubstantiated. The official investigation found no evidence of foul play.

claims.claim: The crash was intentional, orchestrated by external forces.
claims.evidence: Conspiracy theories often implicate intelligence agencies or rival royal families, citing potential political or economic motives.
claims.criticism: This claim is highly speculative and lacks any supporting evidence. It relies on conjecture and unfounded accusations without any verifiable basis.

keyFigures.name: Gilles-Éric Séralini
keyFigures.role: French scientist who questioned the official cause of death.

keyFigures.name: Robert Daley
keyFigures.role: Author of 'Princess Grace,' which presents the official account of the events.

timeline.event: Grace Kelly is born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
Grace Kelly marries Prince Rainier III of Monaco.
Princess Grace dies after a car accident the previous day.

sources.author: Jeffrey Robinson
sources.work: Rainier and Grace: An Intimate Portrait
sources.title: Jeffrey Robinson

sources.author: Various online forums and websites
sources.work: Discussion threads and articles promoting conspiracy theories
sources.title: Various online forums and websites
